news.title: India Accelerator to invest $5 million to double footprint in next 12-18 months
news.para: India Accelerator is set to invest $5 million to grow its coworking spaces across India. The company plans to double its presence in cities within the next 12 to 18 months. New centers are planned for Vadodara, Kolkata, Guwahati, and Bengaluru by the first half of 2026. This expansion aims to enhance community and coworking facilities nationwide.

news.title: Lucknow civic body's budget 2026 okays no hike in property tax
news.para: Lucknow Municipal Corporation approved a Rs 1,253 crore budget for 2026-27, focusing on development without increasing property tax or parking charges. The civic body aims to boost revenue through better tax collection and expanding the tax base, with funds allocated for infrastructure improvements, sanitation, and beautification projects across all wards.

news.title: SWAMIH Investment Fund II in process of being formally launched: Finance minister
news.para: The SWAMIH Investment Fund II is set to launch, aiming to help homebuyers with stuck investments.  The SWAMIH Fund has already committed to 148 projects, delivering over 63,000 homes.  This initiative, established in November 2019, provides crucial debt financing to revive stalled affordable and mid-income housing projects across India.  It acts as a vital support for developers facing difficulties.

news.title: Over 500 objects to proposed collector guideline rates for property in Indore
news.para: Nearly 500 objections were filed against proposed collector guideline rate hikes in Indore, with citizens and experts deeming increases unrealistic. Concerns include blanket hikes for bypass-adjacent land, outdated apartment valuations, and prohibitive costs for villages near new road projects. The District Evaluation Committee will review these complaints before forwarding the final proposal to Bhopal.

news.title: Haryana rights body seeks report on death of seven workers at Gurugram construction site collapse
news.para: The Haryana Human Rights Commission has issued notices to officials following the death of seven workers in a Gurugram construction site collapse. The commission is seeking reports on safety compliance, accountability, and compensation, emphasizing the need for stricter enforcement of labour safety laws to protect workers' fundamental human rights.

news.title: Allotment of plots in new industrial areas of Indore region to begin from April
news.para: The Madhya Pradesh Industrial Development Corporation will begin allotting plots in nine new industrial areas across the Indore region from April. These developments aim to attract investments in sectors like food processing, confectionery, and plastics, bolstering MSME-driven industries. The expansion includes a large township at Bardari and several smaller clusters to support business growth.

news.title: Jaipur development body unveils five land pooling schemes
news.para: The Jaipur Development Authority (JDA) has proposed five new land pooling schemes to support urban expansion, including industrial, residential, and mixed-use developments. These plans span areas along Mahal Road, Agra Road, Kalwar Road, and near Tonk Road, covering significant land parcels for diverse development needs.

news.title: Supreme Universal to invest ₹1,500 crore to develop residential project in Pune
news.para: Supreme Universal is set to invest ₹1,500 crore in a new residential project in Pune's Punawale. The development, named Supreme Rivana, will span 12.6 acres and offer over 2,200 apartments.  Construction will occur in phases, with the investment covering all building costs.

news.title: Marvel Ribera: The design philosophy behind Pune’s new riverside luxury address
news.para: Marvel Ribera presents a new residential address on Boat Club Road, Pune. This development emphasizes international architecture and nature-led design. Homes offer villa-like openness and uninterrupted river views. Residents enjoy enhanced privacy, safety, and convenience. The project includes five-star lifestyle amenities. Marvel Ribera aims to elevate everyday living with a focus on quality of life.

news.title: Buildings in Indore given 15 days to ensure compliance with fire safety norms
news.para: Indore district administration has issued a 15-day ultimatum to G+3 and multi-storey buildings to comply with fire safety norms. Special teams will inspect buildings for operational fire extinguishers, sprinklers, and clear emergency exits. Non-compliant structures face punitive action, including licence cancellation and demolition of illegal expansions.

news.title: Affordable flats at five sites for urban poor: Odisha minister
news.para: Odisha is undertaking five major affordable housing projects in Bhubaneswar, Cuttack, and Rourkela through public-private partnerships. These initiatives, following the Housing for All (Urban) policy, will deliver over 5,000 dwelling units for economically weaker and lower-income groups, addressing the growing demand for low-cost housing in urban areas.

news.title: Existing land use survey drags, Nagpur development plan in doldrums
news.para: Nagpur's development plan faces significant delays as the Existing Land Use survey remains incomplete in over half of the expanded municipal areas. The Nagpur Municipal Corporation is struggling to reconcile land-use details from the Nagpur Improvement Trust, impacting the plan's adherence to statutory timelines. The municipal commissioner has set a new deadline of May 31 for completing the survey.

news.title: Goa consumer commission orders Dimension Builders to form flat owners' society
news.para: A builder must form a society for flat owners in Dimension Residency within three months. If the builder fails, residents can form it themselves. This order comes after a flat owner complained about defects. The commission noted the builder's failure to address issues. Compensation was denied due to the complaint being filed late.

news.title: Bombay HC quashes DRT's stay order on post-sale process of mortgaged flat in Mumbai auctioned by bank
news.para: The Bombay High Court has overturned a Debt Recovery Tribunal order that halted the sale of a south Mumbai flat. The court noted a concerning trend where borrowers delay action until auctions are finalized. They then use insolvency proceedings to stall possession. This ruling provides relief to auction purchasers.

news.title: Global investors eye GIFT City amid West Asia conflict
news.para: Rising geopolitical tensions in West Asia are prompting global investors and NRIs to reassess their Dubai exposure, leading to increased inquiries for India's GIFT City. Investors are seeking regulatory stability and proximity to growth markets, driving a 'dual-hub' strategy rather than a complete relocation from Dubai.

news.title: Embassy REIT targets 13 million sq ft acquisitions to scale income
news.para: Embassy Office Parks REIT is evaluating a 12-13 million sq ft pipeline, including 4 million sq ft from its sponsor, to scale its portfolio and enhance income visibility. The REIT has raised Rs 9,800 crore in FY26 and has a Rs 4,000 crore development pipeline, aiming for yield-accretive Grade A assets.

news.title: Telangana government amends building rules, expands TDR use
news.para: Telangana government has amended building rules, streamlining TDR usage and relaxing parameters to boost real estate. High-rise definitions are updated, and TDR can now be used for setback relaxations and to offset development charges for altered master plan roads. These changes aim to provide greater flexibility and financial ease for developers.

news.title: ED flags Indians buying Dubai properties using credit cards
news.para: Indian authorities are scrutinizing residents who bought Dubai properties using credit cards. This method may breach foreign exchange rules. Individuals are receiving notices from the Enforcement Directorate. They face penalties and the need to regularize transactions. Some may have to sell properties. This situation highlights the complexities of cross-border property investments.

news.title: Manufacturing push drives property developers to build industrial townships
news.para: Property developers are increasingly focusing on integrated industrial townships to support India's manufacturing growth ambitions. This strategic shift aims to create cohesive ecosystems with housing and social infrastructure alongside industrial land, positioning industrial real estate as a stable investment avenue.

news.title: Hyderabad's 'far west' is booming, but is the growth sustainable?
news.para: Hyderabad's western outskirts are experiencing a rapid surge in housing projects, particularly in areas like Kollur and Tellapur, with prices doubling in five years. Despite tempting offers, these 'budget housing' hubs often lack basic amenities, raising concerns about oversupply and potential project stagnation.
